title: ED pediatric injury documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.
red_flags.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC trauma history supports onset, mechanism, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and caregiver concerns for pediatric injury without diagnosing the injury.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured

examination.text: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.
examination.rationale: The exact secondary survey supports measured vital data, manual focused examination, injury severity, appearance, time-critical findings and existing tests only when assessed or reviewed.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured

examination.text: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.
examination.rationale: The exact NICE section supports consent to examination, child-centred communication and completed risk or consent discussion only when performed.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports child-centred, private and accessible communication, consent to examination, and recording actual words and disagreements.

referral.text: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.
referral.rationale: The exact child-assessment section supports documenting safeguarding concern, clinician plan, specialist discussion and arranged handover only when concern was assessed and action completed; it does not assert abuse.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports observing the child and interactions, communicating directly and privately where appropriate, non-leading exploration and structured family or care-environment context.

sources_used.title: Child abuse and neglect
sources_used.jurisdiction: England and Wales; UAE safeguarding law and pathways govern local use

source_limitations: Age, developmental stage, exact mechanism, witness account, delay, injury pattern, neurovascular status, head/neck injury, imaging, diagnosis, treatment and local safeguarding action remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
